page: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 Intel Xeon virtual CPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with 16 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 1280 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier across most standardized tests. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1451.59, while the multi-core CPU performance reaches 21535.52. PassMark CPU and memory scores are 21511.0 and 2031.88 respectively, both falling within the middle 50% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is measured at 107.12 GB/sec for large block reads and 53.71 GB/sec for large block writes. However, single-threaded gzip compression is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 23.39 GB/sec, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average rate of 66.52 GB/sec.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the vc2-16c-64gb is its shared CPU allocation, which can introduce resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances. This shared model offers a cost-effective path for resource-dense deployments requiring substantial memory and storage without the premium of dedicated hardware. With average database operation scores of 4649.2 and a Redis SET throughput of 23.04 million operations per second, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate database or development environments that do not require sustained, non-shared CPU performance.
description: The Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is an x86_64 Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 16 Intel Xeon shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD. Lacking a GPU, this instance relies on its shared CPU architecture, which delivers average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 21535.52 and a PassMark CPU score of 21511.0. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, single-threaded gzip compression represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads by pairing a large memory capacity and substantial local NVMe storage with a shared CPU model. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving with 12.01 GB/sec throughput, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a Cloud Compute server featuring 16 Intel Xeon shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers balanced, average-tier performance for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vc2-16c-64gb is a Cloud Compute server with 16 Intel Xeon shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1280 GB NVMe SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server offering balanced memory and local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 16 Intel Xeon shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 1280 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Tradeoff of shared CPU allocation and weaker single-threaded compression
Suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and development
